ADB · Telangana
Young India Integrated Residential Schools

Overview of nine schools visited.

Findings from field visits across all four welfare societies — Minority, Social, Tribal and Backward Classes. The residential model provides marginalised children with housing, meals and schooling from age 10 to 18. Across the visits, the recurring gaps are mostly in living conditions rather than teaching.

Where we visited

Recurring themes across the visits

Ten themes drawn from all nine profiles, with the specific findings, the schools they came from, and what people told us on the ground.

From the visits

Young India Integrated Residential Schools

Field-visit assessments of welfare residential schools.

A consolidated record of field visits across all four welfare societies — Minority, Social, Tribal and Backward Classes. Each profile documents the school's staffing, infrastructure, enrolment and key findings from the visit.
